concept: Send / Dispatch / Cause to Travel to a Recipient
description: To send: a letter, a gift, word ahead of one's arrival. Causing a thing to travel where one does not go oneself. Completes the transfer verbs: 'loa' (give, in presence), 'sepho' (give, at distance), 'howela' (receive), 'toloi' (messenger).
sound_symbolism: 'se' releases outward with a hiss of motion, 'pho' is the breath-push of dispatch — letting a thing go with direction.
grammatical_notes: With recipient: 'mia wei melu shelu sepho.' (I send a book to my friend.) Word itself: 'shia wei sila nophi to sepho.' (They sent word to the community.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: A future of distributed communities runs on things well sent: seed stock, knowledge, help dispatched where it is needed.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The sent thing carried the sender's trust across distance — messenger, letter, and gift were the village's long arms.
